title: Cinematic Visual Director – Animation
description: Important Note: This is a third-party, client-based position. All details regarding salary, working hours, and other terms will be provided directly by the client during the interview process. Please apply only if you are comfortable with these terms. Additionally, an initial screening form will be shared as the first step of the process. Only those who are genuinely interested in completing the form should apply. If anyone feels this might be a scam or fraud, they are kindly requested not to apply. The process is transparent, and only serious candidates will be considered About the Role We are seeking an experienced and highly creative Art Director to lead the cinematic visual direction of an episodic 3D animated television series developed in Unreal Engine. This role is ideal for someone with strong expertise in real-time cinematic production, virtual cinematography, environment aesthetics, and episodic storytelling workflows. The ideal candidate combines artistic leadership with advanced Unreal Engine technical proficiency and has a strong understanding of cinematic language, shot composition, lighting, camera movement, and visual continuity across multiple episodes. You will collaborate closely with directors, storyboard artists, Unreal Engine artists, animators, and technical teams to help shape the visual identity and storytelling style of the production. Key Responsibilities Lead the overall visual style and artistic direction of the animated series Define cinematic mood, composition, lighting, framing, pacing, and visual continuity Collaborate with directors, storyboard artists, Unreal artists, animators, and technical teams Guide shot design, camera movement, lens selection, and scene staging Supervise cinematic workflows inside Unreal Engine using Sequencer and virtual camera tools Review layouts, lighting passes, environments, animation shots, and final sequences Maintain artistic consistency across episodic production Create and oversee mood boards, style frames, look development, and visual references Help establish scalable artistic pipelines for fast-paced episodic production Troubleshoot rendering and optimization issues for real-time performance Coordinate effectively with small remote teams of artists across the U.S. Required Qualification: Strong professional experience as an Art Director, Cinematic Lead, Visual Development Lead, or Senior Visual Artist in animation, games, VFX, or virtual production Expert-level proficiency in Unreal Engine Advanced experience with: Unreal Sequencer Virtual cameras Cinematic lighting Real-time rendering workflows Scene optimization and rendering troubleshooting Strong understanding of: Cinematic camera language Composition and shot continuity Visual storytelling Episodic production workflows Experience working on episodic 3D productions requiring quick turnaround and repetition Experience collaborating with distributed creative teams Strong communication and leadership skills Additional Required Experience Candidates must have substantial experience with: Slack collaboration workflows Microsoft Azure Cloud Perforce version control (P4V specifically) Generative AI text-to-video tools (please specify tools used, e.g. Sora) Small remote creative teams (maximum 4 artists) Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets Responding to or contributing to RFPs (Request for Proposals) Preferred Qualifications Experience on animated television or streaming productions Storyboarding, previs, layout, or cinematic blocking experience Experience directing virtual camera shoots Familiarity with motion capture or virtual production workflows Experience supervising freelance or distributed teams Application Requirements Please submit the following: Update Resume/CV Portfolio or demo reel

title: Senior Director - Content & Brand Marketing
description: About the Role: Our client is seeking a visionary and strategic Senior Director, Content & Brand Marketing to lead the development and execution of impactful brand storytelling, integrated campaigns, and content strategies across multiple consumer touchpoints. This role will play a critical part in shaping and elevating the brand voice while driving awareness, engagement, and international growth. The ideal candidate is both a creative and strategic leader with deep expertise in brand marketing, content strategy, campaign development, and audience engagement. This individual will ensure all brand communications align with the company’s purpose, positioning, and long-term global expansion goals. Key Responsibilities: Brand Strategy & Leadership Lead and evolve the overall brand marketing strategy to support business objectives and international growth initiatives. Ensure consistency of brand voice, identity, and messaging across all channels and consumer touchpoints. Partner with executive leadership to define and execute long-term brand positioning strategies. Translate brand purpose into compelling campaigns and storytelling initiatives that resonate with target audiences. Content Strategy & Storytelling Develop and oversee best-in-class content strategies across digital, social, editorial, email, retail, and campaign platforms. Lead the creation of compelling brand narratives that strengthen customer connection and engagement. Oversee editorial calendars, campaign messaging, creative briefs, and cross-channel content execution. Ensure content reflects evolving consumer trends, cultural relevance, and brand objectives. Campaign Development & Execution Lead integrated marketing campaigns from concept through execution and performance analysis. Collaborate with creative, e-commerce, PR, social media, and product teams to deliver cohesive brand experiences. Drive seasonal launches, brand activations, partnerships, and global campaign initiatives. Manage agency relationships, production partners, and creative vendors. Audience Growth & Engagement Develop initiatives that increase brand awareness, customer loyalty, and community engagement. Utilize consumer insights, analytics, and market research to optimize campaign effectiveness. Identify new opportunities for audience development and brand expansion across international markets. Support customer acquisition and retention strategies through impactful storytelling and content experiences. Leadership & Team Development Build, mentor, and inspire high-performing creative and marketing teams. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and performance-driven culture. Establish KPIs and reporting frameworks to evaluate content and campaign success. Present strategic recommendations and performance insights to senior leadership. Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, Business, or a related field. 10+ years of experience in brand marketing, content strategy, editorial, or integrated marketing leadership roles. Proven experience developing successful multi-channel brand campaigns and storytelling initiatives. Strong understanding of content marketing, digital platforms, social media, and consumer engagement strategies. Experience leading cross-functional teams and managing agency partnerships. Exceptional communication, creative direction, and leadership abilities. Strong analytical mindset with experience leveraging performance data to optimize campaigns. Preferred Qualifications: Experience within luxury, fashion, lifestyle, beauty, retail, or consumer brands preferred. Global or international brand marketing experience is highly desirable. Familiarity with performance marketing, CRM, and e-commerce content strategies. Strong understanding of evolving digital and cultural trends shaping modern consumer behavior. What Success Looks Like: Elevated global brand awareness and audience engagement. Consistent and impactful brand storytelling across all
score_reason: Senior creative leadership role with strong brand scope, but no salary data, no work arrangement specified, and unclear location/timezone flexibility for Costa Rica transition.

title: VP of Marketing - Head of Marketing
description: About Us Hyprwork is a fast-growing operator of direct-to-consumer brands in health and wellness. Our flagship brand, Rejuvacare, serves over 400,000 customers who trust us with their health and their money. We ship primarily to the United States, offer a 90-day money-back guarantee, and run a RejuvaCare+ membership program that powers recurring revenue. The company has scaled 12x in a single year to nearly 150 people across 15+ countries. We are remote-first, async-first, and hold a high standard of accountability across everything we do. The Role This is the seat that runs marketing for Hyprwork as the company transitions from a founder-led operation into a multi-brand holding. You replace the operational footprint of our outgoing CMO, who moves into a founder and visionary role alongside our CEO. You own marketing strategy, execution, and P&L for the entire Hyprwork portfolio, today centered on Rejuvacare and expanding into new brands over the next four to eight quarters. This is not a strategy-only seat or a corporate CMO post. Hyprwork is a performance-first DTC business that has scaled 12x in a single year on paid acquisition discipline, brand execution, and operational excellence. The CMO here is expected to know the numbers, run the team hands-on, and build the structure that lets marketing scale beyond the founders' direct involvement. You inherit a marketing organization with an established performance marketing function (paid acquisition under five channel pod leads), a creative production engine producing hundreds of assets per week, and developing brand and lifecycle capabilities. You own the design of the next layer of marketing leadership and the build-out as the brand portfolio expands. The founder transition is the defining context of this role. Our founders are stepping back from day-to-day marketing operations but remain deeply invested in brand direction, portfolio strategy, and company outcomes. This is not a clean handover into a vacuum. You are stepping into a seat previously operated by a founder who is becoming your closest strategic partner. The first three to six months are a trust-building phase. You will collaborate closely, demonstrate judgment, and earn the decision rights of the seat before operating fully independently. Comfort with that dynamic, the patience to navigate it, and the maturity not to mistake collaboration for interference are non-negotiable. What You Will Own Marketing Strategy and P&L You own marketing strategy across the Hyprwork portfolio: brand direction, channel mix, budget allocation, customer acquisition and retention strategy, and the P&L for marketing spend. You set the annual and quarterly marketing plans, defend them in the executive leadership team, and deliver against them. Performance Marketing and Growth Paid acquisition is the engine of this business. You own the performance marketing function strategically, with five channel pod leads executing tactically across Meta, Google, Native, AppLovin, and TikTok. You set acquisition targets, CAC and LTV thresholds, channel diversification strategy, and the operational standards that make paid acquisition sustainable as the business scales. You read the dashboards, know the numbers, and challenge them. Creative Function You own the creative production machine that feeds every paid channel. A Creative Manager leads the function with sub-leads across video editing, AI video, graphic design, and copy. The team produces 400+ videos and 250+ images per week to feed the testing system. You hold the Creative Manager accountable for throughput, win rate, and quality. You set the creative strategy that gives the pod leads the at-bats they need to scale revenue. Brand, Lifecycle, and Customer Marketing You own how Hyprwork's brands show up across every customer touchpoint and how they compound value beyond the first purchase. This includes brand identity and standards, lifecycle and retention programs, RejuvaCare+ membership market
score_reason: Senior CMO-equivalent role at remote-first company with strong creative + performance scope, but DTC/performance-marketing focus and missing salary/currency details create minor friction against Travis's brand/integrated campaign background.

title: VP, Global Head of DTC
description: About Harry’s Harry’s is a men’s grooming brand that offers high quality shave, body, hair, and skin care products at an exceptional value. Launched in 2013, Harry’s redefined the shaving experience and helped pioneer the earliest era of direct to consumer in the process. Since then, the brand has become the #2 shave brand globally and expanded to offer products for every step in its customer’s routine. Harry’s is also on a mission to ensure guys have access to quality mental health care. To date, the brand has helped connect over 2 million men to quality mental health resources. Harry’s is part of Mammoth Brands, the modern CPG company behind category-leading brands Harry’s, Flamingo, Lume, Mando, and Coterie. Driven by a mission to “Create Things People Like More,” the company is creating a new model—and home—for brands, founders, and talent looking to solve unmet needs, improve peoples’ lives and ultimately change the status quo. Even as we grow, we take extra care to maintain the small, scrappy, entrepreneurial culture that helped to get us where we are today: a company that people like more, that better serves its customers, employees, and community. Mammoth Brands is committed to making a positive impact and donated over $20 million through our network of nonprofit partners to date. About The Team The Global Digital Commerce organization is responsible for shaping the vision, growth, and execution of all digital businesses across Harry’s, Flamingo, Mando, and Lume . We design and deliver digital experiences that connect our customers to our products, while building the infrastructure that supports long-term omnichannel success. Our team operates at the intersection of brand, customer, innovation, and technology. DTC is the heartbeat of our business - where we launch, learn, and build communities. Where we drive product innovation and interface directly with our customers, learning from their experiences to continually build and connect the brand. We’re accountable for the overall end-to-end customer experience.From marketing to site experience/checkout, to distribution and customer service, and wrapping all those analytics/insights together to bring lessons back into the overall Brand strategy. Ultimately, the team owns the P&L across multiple direct-to-consumer businesses, ensuring each brand thrives digitally while sharing best practices, platforms, and capabilities across the portfolio. Together, we bring innovation, consistency, and scale to Mammoth Care’s digital ecosystem—while keeping the customer at the center. About The Role We’re looking for a seasoned ecommerce/DTC executive to serve as VP, Head of Global DTC , overseeing our flagship brands: Harry’s, Flamingo, Mando, and Lume . This leader will set the strategy for DTC businesses across all our sites globally, with the vast majority in the US, and ensure each brand’s digital experience reflects its unique identity and differential focus on each Brand’s customers while benefiting from shared enterprise capabilities. As the omnichannel landscape evolves amidst a rapidly changing environment, this leader will chart the future evolution of the role of DTC across each brand. You will directly lead leaders (including the DTC heads for each brand) and inspire a global team spanning multiple teams (e.g. UX, marketing, engineering, supply chain/distro, and customer service). Furthermore, as a senior member of the company, you’ll be accountable for business results across all four brands, representing digital commerce at the highest levels of the organization. What You Will Accomplish Define the Multi-Brand DTC Strategy: Build and execute a global strategy that scales DTC across Harry’s, Flamingo, Mando, and Lume while respecting their unique positioning and customer bases. Deliver Across Portfolios: Own the KPIs for all brand DTC channels, aligning strategies with broader omnichannel goals. Lead & Develop Senior Leaders: Manage the Heads of DTC for each brand, fosteri
score_reason: Senior DTC leadership with brand scope, but no mention of remote work, compensation, or location flexibility creates meaningful friction against Travis's Costa Rica move.

title: Head of Creative Services
description: The Head of Creative Services at Highsnobiety will spearhead the development of culturally relevant, creatively ambitious work across brand partnerships, content, social, and experiential. This role is responsible for overseeing our creative teams and shaping how brands show up through the Highsnobiety lens - translating audience behavior, cultural insight, and strategic thinking into breakthrough creative ideas. Working closely with strategy, editorial, sales, production, social, and activation teams, the Head of Creative Services will oversee integrated campaigns from pitch through execution. From branded content and editorial storytelling to live experiences and social-first formats, this role will help define the next generation of Highsnobiety’s creative output while mentoring and leading a multidisciplinary creative team. Your Mission Creative Leadership & Vision: Lead the creative development of integrated campaigns across content, social, experiential, editorial, and branded storytelling. Ensure all work feels culturally relevant, strategically sharp, and distinctly Highsnob. Concept Development: Oversee the ideation and execution of breakthrough creative concepts for brand partners - spanning everything from social-first content series and editorial franchises to experiential activations and cultural moments. Pitch Leadership & New Business: Lead the creative development for major partnership pitches and proactive opportunities. Craft compelling narratives and presentation materials that translate strategic insights into bold, sellable ideas. Team Management & Mentorship: Manage and inspire a multidisciplinary team of creatives including art directors, designers, and freelance talent. Foster a collaborative creative culture rooted in curiosity, experimentation, and a strong sense of craft. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with strategy, editorial, production, social, sales, and activation teams to ensure ideas are executed cohesively across all touchpoints and channels. Client Leadership: Serve as a senior creative voice in client conversations and presentations. Build trusted relationships with partners by clearly articulating creative thinking and guiding projects from kickoff through execution. Cultural Fluency & Innovation: Maintain a strong pulse on fashion, music, internet culture, entertainment, sport, design, and emerging creative trends. Bring fresh perspectives and innovative storytelling approaches that keep Highsnobiety at the forefront of culture. Creative Oversight: Provide hands-on oversight throughout the creative process - from initial concepting through production and launch - ensuring a consistently high standard of creative quality and execution. Editorial & Platform Thinking: Champion ideas that feel native to modern media ecosystems. Understand how storytelling differs across editorial, social, experiential, and creator-led environments, and develop concepts designed to travel organically across channels. Business Impact: Help drive growth across the business through strong creative thinking, successful pitches, and culturally resonant work that delivers meaningful results for both clients and Highsnobiety. Your Skills 12+ years of experience in a senior creative role within an agency, media company, creative studio, or brand. Proven experience leading integrated campaigns spanning content, social, experiential, and cultural marketing. Exceptional presentation and storytelling skills, with the ability to confidently pitch ideas to senior clients and stakeholders. Deep understanding of contemporary culture and how audiences engage across platforms. Experience managing and mentoring creative teams across multiple disciplines. Strong conceptual thinker with a portfolio that demonstrates both strategic rigor and creative originality. Ability to balance big-picture creative ambition with fast-moving timelines and evolving business needs. Collaborative leadership style with experience wo
score_reason: Senior creative leadership with integrated campaign scope, but no remote/location/compensation details stated—unclear if it meets Travis's must-haves.

All of these values have played their part in making IWC Schaffhausen a dynamic and quickly growing international brand which has established itself in the upper echelons of the luxury watch segment: the perfect environment for those embarking on careers in the luxury goods industry as well as highly qualified, ambitious specialists and management figures who are attracted by the flair of the international luxury goods sector. As Head of Communication for North America, you will shape how our Maison connects with press, clients and partners at every touchpoint, orchestrating the activities around brand image and brand awareness while respecting the Maison identity and vision, and creating the best conditions to support business within the Market. Your mission is to strengthen brand equity, deepen engagement, and fuel business growth through seamless, personalized campaigns and activations. Responsibilities Strategy & Planning Lead and develop the annual comprehensive Communications strategy with the MarCom VP, aligning with the long-term business objectives of the market. Support the Marketing & Communications VP in the definition of the brand vision for the region and support on the annual Strategic Plan preparation. Create local Communications/Campaigns calendar in partnership with HQ and Communications team. Provide strategic input to HQ for the development of locally impactful communications concepts, assets and partnerships. Support HQ-lead activation located in North America. Media / Content Manage the Media team in developing and executing the regional media plan (USA & Canada) to enhance brand visibility. Oversee media agency and maintain relationships with key media partners. Explore new media opportunities (online & offline). Ensure strategic visibility on social media, digital publications and digital platforms of all commercial partners. Oversee local content creations projects in collaboration with Media Manager. Wholesale Co-Op Plans Define and execute all regional Co-Op Media Plans in partnership with Media manager and other Marketing departments. Establish strong relationship with all key wholesale partners in the region (External Boutiques and Multibrand). Press & Public Relations / Talents Management Develop and implement regional press strategy in collaboration with the PR team, in line with brand strategy and global communications campaigns to maximize brand visibility and appreciation Ensure dispatch and strong influx of relevant information to key media and retailers ; ensure qualitative and quantitative press coverage (print and digital) Coordinate public relations activities: press-related events for new launches, press trips, shootings and talents dressing. Oversee press agency and maintain relationship with key editors and KOLs. Develop and execute a locally relevant Talents / Celebrities engagement plan, aligning with HQ on global vision and practice Recommend product placements in photoshoots, cinema/TV productions, events and social media. title: Director, Marketing Engineering & AI
description: We are a global team of innovators and pioneers dedicated to shaping the future of observability. At New Relic, we build an intelligent platform that empowers companies to thrive in an AI-first world by giving them unparalleled insight into their complex systems. If you're ready to help the world's best companies optimize their digital applications, we invite you to explore a career with us! Your opportunity New Relic’s Growth Team is looking for a Director, Marketing Engineering & AI who will report to the VP, Growth Marketing Operations and Insights. This leader will be at the forefront of driving scale and efficiency across the entire marketing organization by spearheading the transition to AI-forward tooling and workflows. You will lead a team of technical marketers from the front, overseeing the implementation of new technology and process, guiding the broader adoption throughout the organization. In addition, you will be managing day-to-day campaign operations in support of marketing team initiatives, ensuring that our technical infrastructure effectively powers our growth strategies. A successful leader in this role is a collaborative, strategic problem solver with a heavy dose of AI leadership. You are great at collaborating with stakeholders both inside and outside of marketing to achieve an excellent prospect and customer experience that also balances the desired business result. Demonstrated successful experience in leading technical implementations and driving organizational change is essential. The opportunity to work from a remote office may be available depending on the applicant location. What You’ll Do As a pivotal member of the Growth and Marketing team, you will: Lead Marketing Campaign Operations: Oversee the technical execution of campaign operations to support key marketing team initiatives and drive measurable results. Drive AI Strategy & Implementation: Evaluate and identify opportunities to use AI and emerging technologies to improve marketing results. Lead the transition to AI-forward workflows to increase scale and efficiency. Lead from the Front: Manage a team of technical marketers through problem solving in service of delivering business requirements and amazing prospect and customer experiences. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with stakeholders across the company to ensure a seamless and excellent experience for both prospects and customers. Optimize Tech Stack: Partner with technology teams across the business to acquire, deploy, and maintain key marketing systems, ensuring they are integrated and optimized for revenue growth and business profitability. This role requires 8-10+ years of experience in marketing technology and marketing campaign operations. B2B Experience: Proven track record in a B2B enterprise technology or SaaS environment. AI Leadership: Demonstrated experience transitioning operations and B2B marketing teams to AI-forward tooling and workflows using Claude and/or other AI tools. Account Based Approach: Demonstrated success with ABM including (but not limited to) account scoring and modern account forward tools and marketing tactics. Technical Implementation: Proven success in leading technical teams through complex implementations and driving broad organizational adoption. Stakeholder Management: Exceptional ability to collaborate with and influence stakeholders inside and outside of marketing. Technology Expertise: Hands-on experience with key technologies such as Marketo, Salesforce, and AI-driven marketing tools. Education: Degree in business, marketing, (BS/BA) or equivalent experience. Bonus points if you have Experience with Claude (Cowork, Code, etc) and other workflow or agentic AI tools. Experience in a high-growth digital marketing environment. Experience with product led growth and self service. Experience driving revenue and retention within a large ente

title: Head of Brand Marketing
description: Who are we? Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI. We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers. Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products. Join us on our mission and shape the future! About The Role Cohere is building frontier AI for the enterprise. The product is technical. The buyer is technical. The category is loud. None of that exempts us from being a brand people actually want to be near. We're hiring a Head of Brand Marketing to own how Cohere shows up in the world: visually, verbally, culturally, and emotionally. You'll be the senior leader most responsible for what Cohere feels like in a keynote, on a billboard, in a product launch film, on a t-shirt at a conference, in a post that goes around design Twitter on a Sunday night. The goal isn't brand-as-decoration. It's brand as compounding advantage: the reason a great engineer joins us over a competitor, the reason a CIO remembers our name in a procurement meeting, the reason a journalist picks up our story. This is a senior creative and strategic role. You'll set the brand strategy, own our narrative and verbal identity framework, drive our cultural point of view, and partner closely with two creative counterparts who report into the same leader: our Design Director, who leads in-house design and owns Cohere's visual identity, and our Editorial Director, who leads our owned-media, long-form content, and the execution of voice across what we publish. Together, the three of you are the taste filter for everything that goes out the door under the Cohere name. Your job is to set the vision and the brief; theirs is to bring it to life. The best work will come from real partnership across all three seats. Who You Are You have strong opinions, calmly held, about what's good. You can tell the difference between work that's correct and work that's alive , and you can articulate why. You read widely (design, fashion, music, film, architecture, fiction, technology) not because it's your job to, but because it's how you think. You don't need to come from tech. We'd actually prefer you didn't, fully. The strongest candidates for this role have spent time inside great creative environments: culture-forward consumer brands, world-class agencies, magazines, design studios, record labels, fashion houses, museums. You want to bring that sensibility into a fast-moving B2B AI company. What's non-negotiable is that you can operate inside that environment without losing your taste, and that you can translate a complex AI product into something a human being feels something about. What You'll Own Brand strategy and narrative Set the brand strategy: who Cohere is, what we stand for, what we won't be. Own Cohere's master narrative and make sure every surface (product launches, executive comms, recruiting, sales decks, partner work) ladders up to it without flattening into sameness. Sharpen our positioning at the cultural and emotional level, in tandem with Product Marketing's positioning at the category and competitive level. Verbal identity and creative partnership Own Cohere's verbal identity at the framework level: the voice and tone strategy, copy systems, naming conventions, the brand book. The Editorial Director owns the execution of voice in long-form published work, drawing from the framework you set. Part
score_reason: Senior brand leadership at an AI-forward company with full creative scope (visual, verbal, cultural, emotional)—perfectly aligned with Travis's integrated campaign DNA and AI expertise, though remote status and compensation not explicitly stated.

Proven ability to lead and inspire multidisciplinary creative teams. Strong presentation and client-facing communication skills. A collaborative leader who elevates both the work and the people around them. Actual compensation packages are based on the level of skill and experience each candidate brings to their role. title: Editorial Director
description: Who are we? Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI. We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers. Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products. Join us on our mission and shape the future! About The Role We're hiring an Editorial Director to run Cohere's editorial function. That's the part of the company responsible for everything we publish under our own name, in long form, with our own voice. This isn't a green-field role. You'll inherit an established function with senior people already in seat and meaningful work already going out the door. Your job is to take what's in motion, raise the standard, push the ambition, and give the function a sharper editorial center of gravity. This is not a content marketing role. We don't need someone to scale a case-study library or run a blog calendar tuned to SEO. We need someone who can build a real editorial property. The kind of work people read on a Saturday morning because they want to, not because they were retargeted into it. Think Stripe Press. The early years of Airbnb's magazine. Mailchimp's Courier. MIT Technology Review at its best. A24's editorial output. That register. You'll own Cohere's editorial vision, our publishing calendar, our writers (staff and freelance), and our long-form output across writing, film, photography, podcasts, and eventually books. You'll be a peer to our Head of Brand Marketing and our Design Director. The three of you, reporting to the VP of Brand, set the creative direction of the company. You're our head of storytelling, externally and internally. Externally, you build the editorial property and the body of work we publish under our name. Internally, you help the company tell its own story to itself, so the people making the work can feel what they're a part of and why it matters. The role sits between art and science. The art is editorial taste: knowing what story is worth telling, who should tell it, and what register it lives in. The science is publishing discipline: running a real calendar, shipping on cadence, commissioning and editing at volume without losing the standard, and knowing how a piece actually finds its audience. We need both. People who can do one but not the other will struggle here. Who You Are You're an editor in the old sense of the word. And the new sense too. The old-school part: you can read a draft and tell the writer what it's actually about, including the parts they hadn't realized yet. You commission well. You have a stable of writers, photographers, and filmmakers you trust, and they trust you back. You can talk about a piece of writing at the sentence level and at the strategic level in the same conversation. The new-school part: you think in formats, not just words. A story might want to be a 4,000-word essay, a short film, a photo series, a newsletter, a podcast, a thread. You know which form a story belongs in, and you know how a single idea can travel across surfaces without losing itself in translation. You don't own social (the Social Lead does), but you watch it closely, you understand how it shapes contemporary publishing, and you know that what shows up there is often the public face of the editorial slate. Above all, you're an integrator. You can look at a blog, a newsletter, a film series, a podcast, and a feed and see whether th

From monthly spotlights to the Golden Whisk awards and cooking class perks, our culture blends recognition with hands-on experiences. New team members are welcomed with care and inspired by wellness events, inclusive stories, and a personal touch that makes you feel right at home. Reports to: Chief Marketing Officer Location: Remote (US + Canada) About the Role: Sur La Table needs a strategic CRM leader who can own the full lifecycle of the customer relationship — across email, SMS, loyalty, and customer intelligence — spanning Hardgoods, Culinary programming, and Retail. As Director, Lifecycle Marketing & CRM , you will set direction for how Sur La Table acquires, retains, and grows customer relationships across every business unit, with direct CMO access and meaningful P&L accountability. In your first 6–12 months, you will build the automation architecture to drive significant revenue growth through lifecycle campaigns, establish the customer health reporting cadence that makes CRM performance visible to leadership, and evolve the Sur La Table Perks loyalty program from a discount-driven retention tool into a genuine relationship platform. This is a lean team. You will own a lot, move fast, and make decisions with full end-to-end responsibility. What You'll Do: Email & SMS — BAU / CEP Program Leadership Own the full email and SMS calendar across all business units — cadence, segmentation, deliverability, and send discipline — balancing competing demands from multiple channels and stakeholders. Partner with Brand and Creative to build a library of modular, brand-forward templates that flex across campaign types, business lines, and audience contexts. Lead personalization strategy: dynamic content, audience-specific versioning, and AI-powered content recommendations as standard operating practice. Own core KPIs — revenue per send, repeat purchase rate, LTV by segment, list growth, reactivation rate — and report them to leadership with a clear performance narrative. Automation & Technology Build, scale, and optimize the full automation architecture — post-purchase, lifecycle, Culinary booking, loyalty, and reactivation programs — with a mandate to drive significant revenue growth through automated campaigns. Evaluate and implement best-of-breed CRM technologies. Own the vendor roadmap, build the business case for change, and execute transitions with discipline. AI capability and API/integration depth are primary evaluation criteria. Champion AI-first ways of working: leverage platform integrations and MCP connections to automate insight generation, and partner with internal technology teams to build custom tooling that gives the CRM function a capability edge. Customer Intelligence & CDP Own the CDP and predictive intelligence platforms as strategic assets — driving cross-channel segmentation, LTV modeling, and touchpoint orchestration across all business units. Build the customer health reporting cadence — list trajectory, engagement quality, repeat purchase rate, cross-business-unit buyer rate, LTV cohorts, new customer acquisition, and reactivation performance — and make it visible and actionable for leadership. Loyalty Program Own and evolve the Sur La Table Perks program — structure, benefits, offer architecture, and program economics — with a roadmap that transitions loyalty into a genuine relationship platform. Use customer intelligence to understand what drives frequency, AOV, and cross-business-unit engagement among your best customers. Report customer growth as a recurring leadership metric with a clear narrative around trajectory. Omni-Channel & Cross-Functional Leadership Serve as the CRM voice in cross-functional planning across all business units — GTM strategy, promotional calendar, Culinary programming, and campaign briefing — as an upstream contributor, not a downstream executor. Understand how each
score_reason: Remote + US/Canada + senior scope, but lifecycle/CRM focus is operational marketing, not creative leadership—Travis's strength is brand/integrated creative direction, not customer data platforms.

title: Vice President of Global E-Commerce
description: Vice President of Global E-Commerce — Job Description Job Title: Vice President of Global E-Commerce Location: Los Angeles / Remote Employment Type: Full-time Reports To: Chief Revenue Officer / Chief Marketing Officer About the Role We are seeking an experienced and commercially driven Vice President of Global E-Commerce to lead the company’s worldwide e-commerce strategy, digital revenue growth, online customer experience, marketplace expansion, and direct-to-consumer operations. This role is responsible for building and scaling a high-performing global e-commerce business across regions, channels, platforms, and customer segments. The ideal candidate is a strategic digital commerce leader with strong global market experience, data-driven decision-making capability, and a proven track record of driving online revenue growth in competitive, fast-changing markets. Key Responsibilities Develop and execute the company’s global e-commerce strategy across direct-to-consumer, marketplace, retail media, mobile commerce, social commerce, and digital sales channels. Own global e-commerce revenue growth, conversion performance, digital merchandising, online customer acquisition, retention, and profitability. Lead regional e-commerce teams and ensure alignment between global digital commerce strategy and local market execution. Oversee website performance, digital storefront optimization, product presentation, pricing, promotions, checkout experience, and customer journey improvements. Partner closely with marketing, sales, product, operations, supply chain, finance, technology, customer service, and executive leadership to align e-commerce priorities with business goals. Drive digital growth initiatives including performance marketing, SEO/SEM, CRM, email marketing, affiliate marketing, influencer commerce, personalization, loyalty, and lifecycle marketing. Manage relationships with marketplace partners, platform providers, agencies, technology vendors, payment partners, and logistics providers. Establish e-commerce KPIs, dashboards, reporting systems, and performance review processes for revenue, traffic, conversion, average order value, customer lifetime value, retention, and margin. Identify new digital market opportunities, international expansion strategies, platform partnerships, and emerging commerce trends. Lead initiatives related to site optimization, mobile experience, data analytics, marketing technology, personalization, automation, and digital customer experience. Oversee global e-commerce budgets, forecasts, resource planning, and investment priorities. Ensure e-commerce operations comply with company policies, privacy requirements, payment standards, consumer protection rules, and regional regulations. Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing global e-commerce organization. Maintain confidentiality regarding digital strategy, financial performance, customer data, technology roadmap, and executive-level information. Qualifications 12+ years of progressive experience in e-commerce, digital commerce, online retail, marketplace management, digital marketing, growth, or commercial leadership roles. Proven experience leading global or multi-region e-commerce businesses and delivering measurable online revenue growth. Strong understanding of digital commerce strategy, conversion optimization, merchandising, customer acquisition, retention, marketplaces, and DTC operations. Experience managing e-commerce platforms, digital marketing channels, analytics tools, agencies, vendors, and cross-functional teams. Demonstrated ability to scale online sales, improve customer experience, optimize performance metrics, and drive profitable growth. Strong commercial and financial acumen, including revenue forecasting, margin analysis, budgeting, pricing, and promotional planning. Excellent leadership, executive communication, stakeholder management, and team-building skills. Strong analytical mindset with experience using customer data, fun

title: Senior Empowerment Director
description: Job Title: Senior Director of Revenue Growth Department: Growth Strategy/Revenue Operations/Business Leadership Reporting to: Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) / Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Employment Type: Full-time Level: Senior Leadership Job Overview We are looking for an experienced, results-oriented Senior Director of Revenue Growth to lead a rapidly growing company in leveraging blockchain technology and digital innovation to achieve company-wide revenue growth initiatives. This executive will be responsible for designing and executing scalable revenue strategies, identifying emerging market opportunities, optimizing customer acquisition channels, and driving sustainable growth for blockchain-based products and services. The ideal candidate should possess deep expertise in strategic growth management, revenue operations, business intelligence, digital ecosystems, and blockchain market dynamics. This role requires close collaboration with the executive team, product development team, sales team, marketing team, data analytics team, and strategic partners team. Key Responsibilities Develop and execute corporate revenue growth strategies aligned with company goals and long-term business expansion objectives. Lead cross-functional projects to accelerate revenue growth for products, platforms, and emerging digital services. Discover new business opportunities within the blockchain ecosystem, including: Digital Assets Web3 Platforms Decentralized Finance (DeFi) Smart Contract Applications Tokenization Solutions Enterprise-Grade Blockchain Infrastructure Build and optimize revenue forecasting models, key performance indicators (KPIs), and performance dashboards. Analyze customer behavior, market trends, competitive landscape, and revenue performance metrics. Work with product leadership teams to develop profitable strategies for blockchain-based products and services. Drive pricing optimization initiatives and customer lifetime value (LTV) enhancement projects. Build strategic partnerships with financial institutions, technology providers, and blockchain ecosystem players. Lead demand generation strategies and oversee customer acquisition and retention initiatives. Leverage advanced analytics, AI-driven insights, and blockchain transaction data to improve growth decisions. Submit strategic growth recommendations and business performance reports to senior management and board members. Lead and mentor effective growth, revenue operations, and business development teams. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Finance, Marketing, Economics, Technology Management, or a related field; MBA preferred. 10+ years of leadership experience in revenue growth, business strategy, business operations, or corporate development. Deep understanding of blockchain technology and decentralized ecosystems. Experience in: Revenue Operations (RevOps) SaaS Growth Models Digital Platforms Blockchain Products Marketing Strategies Experience in successfully driving large-scale revenue growth projects. Strong analytical and financial modeling skills. Executive-level communication and stakeholder management skills. Preferred Qualifications Experience in the cryptocurrency, fintech, Web3, or digital asset industry. Understanding of blockchain protocols, smart contracts, and token economics. Familiarity with data platforms, CRM systems, and business intelligence tools. Experience in leading global market expansion projects. Core Competencies Strategic Leadership Revenue Optimization Blockchain Market Insights Business Growth Strategy Data-Driven Decision Making Executive Communication Business Transformation Cross-Functional Collaboration Customer Acquisition and Retention Innovation Management

title: Director, Growth - Health Plan Partnership Strategy
description: Pelago is the world’s leading virtual clinic for Substance Use Management. Our program provides guidance, support and treatment for members seeking to overcome their tobacco, alcohol and opioid use. From unhealthy habits to active substance use disorders, Pelago delivers a personalized solution based on individual health, habits, genetics, and goals, providing care for members wherever they might be on the substance use spectrum. Pelago's suite of virtual services ranges from education, to cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) to comprehensive medication-assisted treatment (MAT). Pelago enables employers and health plans to deliver accessible, affordable, and effective treatment for substance misuse. Pelago has scaled to helping hundreds of employers and health plans and has already helped more than 750,000 members manage their substance use better. We have recently closed our Series C and raised over $151m from leading global investors. If you are passionate about making an impact on the health of others, join us and make it happen! Overview Of The Role... We’re hiring a Director of Growth - Health Plan and Partnership Strategy to own and grow relationships after initial launch. You will manage a portfolio of health plans and partners, driving sustained engagement, generating measurable utilization over time, and driving scalability and strategic initiatives. This role requires strong ownership and rigor, along with a proven ability to drive outcomes within a portfolio. You will lead strategic planning conversations, coordinate cross-functional execution, and ensure each partnership delivers clear, measurable impact. You’ll operate as the owner of your accounts: protecting retention, increasing engagement depth, expanding campaign scope, and translating client commitment into sustained engagement. In this role you will… Own a portfolio of health plan and partner accounts post-launch, service as the primary relationship owner responsible for retention, engagement, and long-term account health. Lead structured business reviews, strategic planning sessions, and annual account planning with external stakeholders to align on goals, surface opportunities, and demonstrate ROI. Identify and pursue growth opportunities within existing accounts increasing campaign scope, broadening stakeholder engagement, and deepening the partnership over time. Coordinate cross-functional execution across internal teams (product, clinical, marketing, operations) to ensure partner commitments are delivered on time and with clear impact. Own performance-based metrics tied to account growth, retention, and utilization; proactively manage risks and escalate where needed to protect and grow the book of business. Navigate complex, multi-stakeholder environments at health plans — building relationships across clinical, operational, and executive levels to drive alignment and sustained adoption. Maintain rigorous pipeline and account hygiene in Salesforce, tracking engagement activity, forecasting renewal and expansion opportunities, and supporting leadership reporting. Contribute to scalability initiatives by identifying patterns across accounts and partnering with internal teams to build repeatable playbooks, processes, and tools. The background we are looking for... 5+ years of experience in B2B roles: account management, customer success, or partnership management, with demonstrated success growing and retaining existing accounts. 5+ years owning performance-based metrics tied to account growth, retention, engagement, or utilization. 5+ years managing external relationships in complex, multi-stakeholder environments Experience leading structured business reviews, strategic planning conversations, or annual account planning with external stakeholders. Along with proven ability to grow relationships in multi-stakeholder environments. Experience using Salesforce, with the ability to manage a sales pipeline, track performance, and support forecasting Experience

title: Senior Director, Membership
description: The American Marketing Association is looking for a Senior Director, Membership! Who We Are About the American Marketing Association (AMA) As the leading global professional marketing association, the AMA is the essential community for marketers. From students and practitioners to executives and academics, we aim to elevate the profession, deepen knowledge, and make a lasting impact. The AMA is home to five premier scholarly journals, including: Journal of Marketing , Journal of Marketing Research , Journal of Public Policy and Marketing , Journal of International Marketing , and Journal of Interactive Marketing . Our industry-leading training events and conferences define future-forward practices, while our professional development and PCM® professional certification advance knowledge. With 70 chapters and a presence on 350 college campuses across North America, the AMA fosters a vibrant community of marketers. The association's philanthropic arm, the AMA’s Foundation , is inspiring a more diverse industry and ensuring marketing research impacts public good. AMA views marketing as the activity, set of institutions, and processes for creating, communicating, delivering, and exchanging offerings that have value for customers, clients, partners, and society at large. You can learn more about AMA’s learning programs and certifications, conferences and events, and scholarly journals at AMA.org . What to know about this role We are a fully remote organization with team members in over 20 states within the US. Work from the comfort of your home, a local coffee shop or wherever in the U.S. brings you joy! However , please note that you MUST live in one of the following states to be considered for this role: Alabama, Colorado, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Louisiana, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, or Wisconsin! We are unable to consider candidates who require an H1-B sponsorship at this time. The salary range for this position is: $120,000 to $130,000 based upon experience. We are unable to negotiate higher than this range. Please note that only applicants who apply officially will be considered in this process. We appreciate the candidates who apply and express interest, but we cannot circumvent the recruitment process for any candidates, regardless of their relationship to the AMA or our team members. The impact of this role The Director, Membership is responsible for understanding, growing, and deepening the membership base of the American Marketing Association. This role sets the vision and strategy for AMA's professional, group, and corporate membership programs, driving acquisition, engagement, and retention initiatives that deliver measurable value to members. The Director leads the development and execution of strategic plans that strengthen AMA's value proposition, expand its reach within the marketing profession, and cultivate a vibrant, growing community of marketing practitioners and leaders. What you’ll be doing Professional, Group & Corporate Membership Growth Sets the vision and leads the development of strategies and plans to grow AMA's professional, group, and corporate membership segments, with clear goals for acquisition, retention, and overall membership health. Develops and refines compelling membership value propositions tailored to each membership segment, ensuring benefits are clearly communicated and consistently delivered. Identifies and pursues new market opportunities to expand the corporate membership program, building relationships with marketing leaders, agencies, and organizations aligned with AMA's mission. Guides the collection, analysis, and reporting of critical membership data—including acquisition, renewal, and churn metrics—to inform strategic and operational decisions. title: Senior Director, Head of Trademarks
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Director, Head of Trademarks in United States. This executive-level role offers the opportunity to lead and shape a global trademark strategy for a highly recognized international brand portfolio. You will oversee complex trademark operations spanning pharmaceutical, consumer, and medical product categories across multiple jurisdictions worldwide. The position combines strategic legal leadership with operational innovation, leveraging modern technologies and AI-powered tools to enhance portfolio management and brand protection efforts. Working closely with cross-functional teams across legal, regulatory, commercial, and R&D functions, you will play a central role in safeguarding intellectual property and supporting long-term business growth. The role also involves leading anti-counterfeit initiatives, advising senior leadership, and driving trademark strategy in a fast-moving global environment. This is an exceptional opportunity for a seasoned trademark attorney who thrives at the intersection of law, innovation, and global brand strategy. Accountabilities Lead and manage a large-scale global trademark portfolio, including pharmaceutical product names, consumer brands, trade dress, and medical or surgical product marks across numerous international jurisdictions. Develop and execute comprehensive trademark strategies covering clearance, prosecution, maintenance, enforcement, and portfolio optimization aligned with commercial priorities. Oversee trademark clearance and naming strategies for new pharmaceutical and consumer products in collaboration with regulatory, marketing, medical, and commercial stakeholders. Implement and enhance AI-driven trademark management processes and automation tools to improve operational efficiency and portfolio oversight. Direct global relationships with external trademark counsel, ensuring strategic consistency, quality, and cost management. Design and lead anti-counterfeit and brand protection initiatives across online marketplaces, social platforms, customs enforcement, and litigation channels when necessary. Serve as the primary advisor on trademark-related matters to senior leadership, marketing teams, R&D, business development, and regulatory functions. Support mergers, acquisitions, and post-acquisition intellectual property integration activities. Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing trademarks team while fostering a proactive and collaborative legal culture. Present portfolio performance, enforcement strategies, and emerging risks to executive leadership and board-level stakeholders. Requirements Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from an accredited law school with active U.S. bar membership required. Minimum of 7 years of trademark law experience, including substantial experience in-house or advising global pharmaceutical, consumer goods, or medical device organizations. Deep expertise in pharmaceutical and consumer product naming, trademark clearance, and global trademark portfolio management. Demonstrated experience managing large and complex international trademark portfolios, ideally exceeding 10,000 marks. Strong understanding of anti-counterfeit enforcement strategies, including online platform enforcement, customs recordation, and related legal actions. Experience leveraging AI-powered trademark tools, automation technologies, or advanced trademark management platforms such as Anaqua. Proven ability to collaborate effectively across legal, commercial, regulatory, marketing, and executive teams. Strong leadership, mentoring, and team management capabilities. Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to advise executive stakeholders on strategic IP matters. Experience supporting mergers, acquisitions, and post-merger IP integration activities in multinational environments is highly valued. Benefits Competitive salary range of approximately $280,000-$320,000

title: Senior Director, Head of Trademarks
description: Bausch + Lomb (NYSE/TSX: BLCO) is a leading global eye health company dedicated to protecting and enhancing the gift of sight for millions of people around the world—from the moment of birth through every phase of life. Our mission is simple, yet powerful: helping you see better, to live better. Our comprehensive portfolio of over 400 products is fully integrated and built to serve our customers across the full spectrum of their eye health needs throughout their lives. Our iconic brand is built on the deep trust and loyalty of our customers established over our 170-year history. We have a significant global research, development, manufacturing and commercial footprint of approximately 13,000 employees and a presence in approximately 100 countries, extending our reach to billions of potential customers across the globe. We have long been associated with many of the most significant advances in eye health, and we believe we are well positioned to continue leading the advancement of eye health in the future. We are seeking a highly accomplished and strategic trademark attorney to serve as Senior Director, Head of Trademarks . This is a rare opportunity to own and shape the global trademark function for one of the world's most iconic eye health brands. You will manage a large, complex portfolio spanning pharmaceutical, consumer, and surgical product categories, partnering closely with brand, regulatory, commercial, and legal teams across the enterprise. This role reports to senior legal leadership and carries significant influence over brand protection strategy globally. Leverage AI-powered tools and automation platforms to drive efficiency, reduce cost, and improve quality in portfolio management, watch services, and prosecution workflows. Oversee and direct relationships with outside counsel globally, managing cost and ensuring strategic alignment. Direct enforcement actions including cease-and-desist campaigns, platform takedowns, Customs recordation, and, where appropriate, civil litigation or criminal referrals. Collaborate with supply chain, regulatory, and government affairs teams on brand protection and product authentication initiatives. Stay current on emerging counterfeit threats in the ophthalmic pharmaceutical and consumer lens care space and adapt enforcement strategy accordingly. Cross-functional leadership Act as the primary legal advisor on trademark matters to brand, marketing, commercial, regulatory, and R&D teams globally. Partner with M&A and business development teams on IP due diligence and post-acquisition trademark integration. Build and mentor a high-performing internal trademarks team and foster a culture of proactive brand protection. Present regularly to senior leadership and the board on portfolio health, enforcement activity, and emerging risks. title: Senior Visual Brand Designer
description: Senior Visual Designer -  Klimt & Design Klimt & Design is a branding and product design agency specializing in building brands for technology companies from pre-seed and Series A to larger, more mature enterprises. Our clients range from high-growth startups to established global enterprises across sectors such as AI, enterprise SaaS, fintech and healthcare, and our work spans brand creation, rebranding, landing pages, marketing design, and product design. We are seeking a full-time, fully remote, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. The Role We are seeking a full-time, senior-level visual brand designer to join our team. This is a client-facing delivery lead position where you will own multiple accounts end-to-end. You will execute the heavy-lifting strategic brand explorations while managing, mentoring, and delegating production tasks to our highly capable mid-level design team. Responsibilities Take ownership of client visual brand design projects, acting as the senior creative that clients experience as the lead. Lead the exploratory zone of brand systems and refreshes, executing competitive research, mood boards, positioning, and defining look, feel, and tone. Present explorations and application mockups to clients on review calls, guiding the team through incorporating feedback successfully. Delegate the extension of the design system (e.g., sizing ads, building out website pages, creating presentation decks) to mid-level designers. Lead daily team standups and act as the first point of contact for mid-level designers needing direction or unblocking. Own final quality assurance, catching issues at your level and ensuring strict process discipline for file hygiene and versioning. Anticipate and flag project risks (timeline, scope, missing inputs) and come to the table with proposed solutions. Oversee and occasionally execute sales enablement collateral (such as pitch decks and slide templates), defining the visual strategy before delegating the broader build-out to the team. Requirements Proven experience working autonomously in a senior design role, comfortable defining a brand system from very little initial direction. A standout portfolio demonstrating core competency in visual identity, brand design, and excellent creative thinking. High comfort level running client review calls and articulating and defending design decisions. Strong ability to context-switch across multiple accounts – ranging from earlier-stage tech companies to mature clients – at different stages of delivery. Proficiency in Figma and all industry-standard design tools. A strong commitment to mentorship; making the people around you better must be viewed as a core part of the job, not a bonus. Excellent fluency in English (verbal, comprehension, and communication). A mindset of "initiative over permission," where you proactively ask questions to fill gaps and recommend solutions rather than waiting to be told what to do. To apply, please submit a link to your portfolio or a few work samples highlighting your experience as a senior creative lead. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Creative Director, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: Why We Exist More than 80% of Americans own a car and see it as a fundamental necessity, yet the experience of owning one is stuck in the 90s. Every part of the user journey (shopping for a car, insurance, repairs, etc) is complicated, fragmented, and time-consuming, and the costs are staggering. In 2025, the cost of owning a car was more than 20% of the average household income, after tax. At Jerry.ai, we are building the first app to manage it all and make car ownership accessible to all. Why We Need You Since we launched our app in 2019, we’ve reached 5M customers, raised over $240M in funding, scaled our annual revenue more than 70X, and became profitable in early 2024 – just 7 years into our operating history, in spite of challenging macro conditions and in a hyper-competitive $2T U.S. market. We did this with an exceptionally talented team of analytical thinkers who are brilliant at experimentation and growth optimization. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Car ownership is a stressful and emotionally charged topic for most people, and right now, our messaging is very transactional (e.g. click here, shop with us). We need you to change that. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Whether you’re crafting email sequences, push notifications, navigating a subreddit thread, or shaping how we talk about a new feature launch, you will be the voice of Jerry. And you will help us earn our brand and cultivate an enduring community. We spent the last 9 years building a product that people like, now we need to give them a reason to trust us and a reason to stay. Why You Will (or Won’t) Like Working Here Blank canvas: You won’t inherit a tone of voice or brand playbook. You will build it in partnership with our CEO, including defining our personality and voice across all organic surfaces. Hands-on: This is a senior level role in scope and impact, but you will be an IC with no direct reports on day one. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. Phase 1: Your sole focus will be overhauling our existing customer communications, including our CRM, email, SMS, and in-app copy. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. You can translate complex, dry, and even stressful topics (car insurance, loans, repairs) into copy that is engaging, human, and accessible. A T-Shaped marketer: Your deep expertise is in copywriting, audience development and product positioning. Horizontally, you have a great sense of the social media landscape and community dynamics (like Reddit). A first principles thinker: You avoid making assumptions. title: Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Retention
description: Why We Exist More than 80% of Americans own a car and see it as a fundamental necessity, yet the experience of owning one is stuck in the 90s. Every part of the user journey (shopping for a car, insurance, repairs, etc) is complicated, fragmented, and time-consuming, and the costs are staggering. In 2025, the cost of owning a car was more than 20% of the average household income, after tax. At Jerry.ai, we are building the first app to manage it all and make car ownership accessible to all. Why We Need You Since we launched our app in 2019, we’ve reached 5M customers, raised over $240M in funding, scaled our annual revenue more than 70X, and became profitable in early 2024 – just 7 years into our operating history, in spite of challenging macro conditions and in a hyper-competitive $2T U.S. market. We did this with an exceptionally talented team of analytical thinkers who are brilliant at experimentation and growth optimization. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Car ownership is a stressful and emotionally charged topic for most people, and right now, our messaging is very transactional (e.g. click here, shop with us). We need you to change that. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Whether you’re crafting email sequences, push notifications, navigating a subreddit thread, or shaping how we talk about a new feature launch, you will be the voice of Jerry. And you will help us earn our brand and cultivate an enduring community. We spent the last 9 years building a product that people like, now we need to give them a reason to trust us and a reason to stay. Why You Will (or Won’t) Like Working Here Blank canvas: You won’t inherit a tone of voice or brand playbook. You will build it in partnership with our CEO, including defining our personality and voice across all organic surfaces. Hands-on: This is a senior level role in scope and impact, but you will be an IC with no direct reports on day one. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. Phase 1: Your sole focus will be overhauling our existing customer communications, including our CRM, email, SMS, and in-app copy. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. You can translate complex, dry, and even stressful topics (car insurance, loans, repairs) into copy that is engaging, human, and accessible. A T-Shaped marketer: Your deep expertise is in copywriting, audience development and product positioning. Horizontally, you have a great sense of the social media landscape and community dynamics (like Reddit). A first principles thinker: You avoid making assumptions. You know how to take qualitative data and user pain points and turn them into resonant, empathetic marketing copy. Comfortable with discomfort: You’ve weathered fast-growing startups before and can confidently push back on highly analytical, data-first teams to champion the unmeasurable va
score_reason: Strong creative mandate (lifecycle messaging, emotional positioning) and likely remote, but DTC/retention focus and unclear seniority level vs. Travis's national CD pedigree create meaningful gaps.

title: Senior Creative Strategist
description: If you have not directly led performance creative that scaled a brand from low–mid 7 figures to 8 or 9 figures in revenue, this role is not a fit. If you have, and want the next big growth + scale challenge, read on. About AutoReel AutoReel is the leading AI-powered platform that transforms real estate listing photos into cinematic property videos in minutes. We serve thousands of users — from independent agents to major brokerages. Our platform enables real estate professionals (realtors, brokers, real estate photographers and media companies) to create professional property videos without the high cost and time associated with traditional video production. We’re a small, fast-moving team that ships constantly, with the leadership team from world-class companies such as Facebook, Instagram, and Snapchat. We’re scaling rapidly and need a creative leader who can build the creative engine that fuels our paid growth across Meta Ads, YouTube, and beyond. About the Role We’re looking for a Head of Performance Creative to own the creative strategy and production behind AutoReel’s paid acquisition. This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll build the systems, build and manage a lean team (primarily offshore), and personally own the creative ads output that powers our growth. You’ll be responsible for producing 50-100+ video ads per week across channels, building scalable creative testing frameworks, and integrating AI tools into every stage of the workflow. This needs to be a thoughtful, scalable system that can support scaling up to 100-500 creatives weekly if needed. You understand how Meta and TikTok algorithms reward creative, and you know how to turn that knowledge into lower CPAs and higher ROAS. This isn’t a “big team, big budget” role. It’s a “scrappy, high-output, build-the-machine” role at a startup that’s growing fast and competing hard. What You’ll Do Own performance creative strategy: Set the creative direction and testing roadmap on Meta. You’ll decide what gets made, why, and how we measure success. Build and run the creative engine: Design the workflows, templates, feedback & learning systems that let us produce 50+ high-quality ad creatives per week without burning out – and support scaling up the number of creatives weekly as we grow. Lead with AI: Integrate AI tools into every stage of creative production — from ideation and scripting to editing and iteration. Maximize throughput while keeping quality high. Manage a small, high-performing team of creatives (as needed) Drive creative testing at scale: Build structured testing frameworks (hooks, formats, messaging angles, audiences) and turn data into actionable creative briefs. Partner with Growth: Work closely with our paid media buyer and creative production team to align creative strategy with campaign performance. Turn spend data into better ads. Know our customers deeply: Understand the real estate agent and photographer psyche — their fears, desires, and buying triggers — and translate that into ads that convert. Qualifications Direct owner or co‑owner of creative for at least one SaaS (or DTC) brand that grew from 7 figures to 8 or 9 figures in ARR/revenue while you were in the seat. 5+ years building and leading creative teams in performance marketing, growth, or digital advertising. Proven track record of producing high-volume performance creative on Meta Deep understanding of how platform algorithms evaluate and reward creative — you think in hooks, thumb-stop rates, and hold rates. Hands-on experience with AI tools and creative tools (video generation, script writing, image editing) and a strong opinion on how to integrate them into production workflows. Experience building creative testing systems and translating performance data into actionable creative strategy. Comfortable in a startup environment where you’ll build processes and systems from scratch, not inherit them. Strong editorial eye and ability to give fast, clear creative feedback. Bonus:
score_reason: Strong performance creative pedigree match and AI integration appeal, but DTC/SaaS performance focus and missing remote/salary details create uncertainty.

Our platform combines the best of AI and human intelligence to help contact centers discover customer insights and behavioral best practices, automate conversations and inefficient processes, and empower every team member to work smarter and faster. Born from the prestigious Stanford AI lab, Cresta's co-founder and chairman is Sebastian Thrun, the genius behind Google X, Waymo, Udacity, and more. Our leadership also includes CEO, Ping Wu, the co-founder of Google Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platform, and co-founder, Tim Shi, an early member of Open AI.
