Our valued customers include brands like Intuit, Cox Communications, Hilton, and Carmax and we’ve been recognized by Forbes and Bain Consulting as one of the top private AI companies in the world. Join us on this thrilling journey to revolutionize the workforce with AI. The future of work is here, and it's at Cresta. About The Role The Senior Brand & Integrated Campaigns Manager is a brand-new role at Cresta. Your job will be to tell our story in creative ways that break through the noise. Part storyteller, part creative driver, and part brand, ad, and integrated campaign strategist, it is your job to leverage all of the cool things happening at Cresta to increase brand awareness, reputation, and growth. You’ll work within a highly talented and creative Marketing organization to dream up, create, and execute on breakthrough campaigns incorporating video, visual, written, and social media content as well as out-of-home advertising. In partnership with the Head of Communications and the CMO, your job is to “own the narrative.” The right candidate will bring their own vision to this high-impact, high-ownership role. This job is perfect for a multi-talented creative thinker who is up for a challenge. title: Lead Strategist – UGC
description: About UGC Factory UGC Factory (www.ugcfactory.io) partners with fast-growing DTC brands to build high-converting, creator-led ads across paid social. We combine real customer journeys, rigorous performance analysis, and tight creative systems to ship UGC that actually moves MER, not just fills a content folder. Role UGC Factory is hiring a Lead Strategist to own the performance of all UGC and statics across our client roster. This person is the de-facto head of creative: the one who sits between data, strategy, scripts, creators, and editors, and is directly accountable for whether the ads work. This role replaces the founder on key client calls, leads ad review, and acts as the final backstop on creative quality and direction. Responsibilities Take end-to-end ownership for why ads work or don't work across multiple accounts. Review performance reports and audits, and distill them into clear creative direction per client. Attend and gradually lead client kickoffs and recurring syncs for key accounts. Translate messy client input into sharp creative strategy, including angles, frameworks, proof requirements, and UGC versus testimonial versus product-seeding plans. Call out and correct misaligned strategies when the creative approach does not fit the offer / SKU Take learnings from performance data and convert them into reusable patterns the team can apply across brands. Ensure those insights actually show up in briefs and not just in a single account silo. Refine AI-generated scripts and frameworks so every approved script reflects a high standard on hooks, angles, and structure. Requirements 3-5+ years driving paid social creative strategy for DTC brands, with significant Meta experience. Strong fluency in Ads Manager and in turning performance data into specific creative hypotheses. Proven ability to lead or heavily influence UGC and creator-led creative across multiple brands at once. Strong scripting instincts and the ability to quickly spot weak hooks, missing proof, and structural issues in UGC and testimonial content. Comfortable being client-facing, running calls, pushing back on misaligned strategies, and owning results. High attention to detail and a habit of catching misaligned frameworks, missing creator data, and bad ideas before they reach the client. Compensation & Commitment Base salary: $60,000–$80,000 Performance incentive: Up to additional $40,000/yr tied to client retention & portfolio winner rate Remote-friendly; available during US business hours

title: Vice President of Production
description: Position Title : VP of Production Division : Production Services ABOUT EPISODE.1 Episode.1 is a full service creative agency focused on delivering best in class brand experiences across the globe. Headquartered in Los Angeles, we are a team of creators and producers that have spent our careers developing experiences that sit at the center of culture. From electrifying music festivals to unforgettable sporting events, these moments fuel our passion. In addition to being part of these moments, our team’s ultimate mission is to pursue meaningful projects with those that share our values and desire to take on opportunities that have limitless potential. OVERVIEW OF POSITION Episode.1 is looking for a Vice President of Production, who is responsible for overseeing the end-to-end execution of high-profile experiential marketing programs, brand activations, live events, pop-ups, mobile tours, retail installations, sponsorship experiences, and large-scale consumer engagements. This leader will manage a group of internal and external production teams, establish operational excellence, and ensure every project is delivered efficiently, safely, on time, and at the highest standard. The VP of Production will partner closely with Creative, Strategy, Client Services, Operations, and agency leadership to bring ambitious brand experiences to life. The ideal candidate is a highly experienced production leader with deep knowledge of experiential marketing, live event production, fabrication, technical production, staffing, budgeting, vendor management, permitting, logistics, and team leadership. As a member of Episode.1’s senior leadership team, this role will be responsible for shaping all aspects of the agency. VP OF PRODUCTION RESPONSIBILITIES: Lead, mentor, and scale an international team of producers across a portfolio of current and future projects Manage at a high level the full production lifecycle from budget development through activation and final reconciliation Establish and enforce production processes, workflows, and best practices across the department Partner with Creative, Account, Strategy and Agency leadership to align production capabilities with agency ambitions and client expectations Build and maintain a robust vendor and freelance network across venues, AV, fabrication, staffing, and logistics Oversee department P&L, including resource allocation, revenue tracking and all financial forecasts Serve as executive escalation point for complex, high-stakes productions and client relationships, while leading productions as needed Develop and manage the annual production department budget and headcount planning Drive innovation in production methodology, ai, technology integration, and sustainability practices Identify potential setbacks/risks, develop contingency plans, and manage dependencies across internal and external teams to maintain delivery momentum. But what we don’t have is someone who obsesses over every single word we put in front of our customers and how we make them feel. Your job will be to think about what our customers actually need (or want) to hear from us, and how to fundamentally change how we speak to and care for them. Car ownership is not “sexy”: Insurance, maintenance, and safety are only topics people think about when they have a bill to pay, a flat tire to change, or when they get into an accident. Anyone can market something inherently exciting, but can you build enthusiasm and trust around a “dry” topic? What You’ll Own We need someone who knows how to talk about a brand in ways that make people feel something and take notice. You will take our messaging from functional and direct to value-additive and relationship-driven. You will work closely with our Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights team to understand how to build trust, engagement, and as a result, drive long-tail conversion. Phase 2: As our tone of voice begins to take shape and we see success with lifecycle campaigns, your scope may expand as you take on driving customer growth and building brand affinity through producing other creative organic content (e.g. social media, etc). Who You Are A copywriting virtuoso: You know that every word matters. title: Fractional CMO
description: FRACTIONAL CMO Plum Good Marketing | adj: very good. worth having.™ Plum Good Marketing is hiring a Fractional CMO. The role goes to someone who has spent five to eight years getting good at owning client relationships and is ready to step into the marketing seat at our clients’ leadership tables. You'll own your clients' marketing strategy. You'll attend their leadership meetings. You'll connect every marketing tactic to a business outcome they actually care about. You’re not someone who takes orders, you take ownership . That's the job. And it’s really fun and rewarding. This is also a development role. You're not expected to walk in carrying a full client load on day one. The first months are training, shadowing, and onboarding into Plum's voice, methodology, and the way we operate inside our clients' businesses. ABOUT PLUM We're a fractional CMO firm based in Metro Detroit. Our clients are growth-ready manufacturers, EOS-aligned leadership teams, and second-generation business owners. They have execution happening everywhere and strategic marketing ownership nowhere. They need us to fill that very important seat. We're technically an agency, but we don’t act like one. We operate inside our clients' businesses as the marketing department they always wished they had. Our seven core values are very well thought out and actionable in everything we do. We came up with them as a team and they're the filters we use for hiring, operating, and choosing clients: We are Reliable : We do what we say. Clients never have to chase us. We are Strategic : Strategy is our love language. Every tactic ties to a business outcome, or we don't do it. We are Experts : We've held our own in big rooms with smart people, and we belong there. When we know something, we say it with conviction. When we don't, we ask without being ashamed. We have EQ : Behind every frustrated client is a business owner who's poured everything into what they've built. We meet them there first. We are Thoughtful : We push back when something isn't in a client's best interest, or when "going above and beyond" has quietly turned into going at our own expense. Thoughtful means intentional, not slow. We are Worth It : We don't deliver work that isn't worth someone's time, attention, or money. Full presence is part of the value, and we know our value. We are Integrative : We don't operate in silos. Plum isn't a vendor you hand a task to. We're the connective tissue between vision and results. We're also operationally obsessed. We’re proud to have clear workflows and actual SOPs. And, we love Asana. We have written etiquette agreements for how we work together. We are utterly allergic to manufactured emergencies and chaos. We work remotely across different schedules. We assume positive intent. We don't manufacture fire drills. We hold each other accountable firmly and kindly. Full presence for our clients starts with full presence for ourselves. We run on EOS internally. Level 10s, rocks, scorecards, V/TO. If those words mean nothing to you yet, that’s ok, you'll learn them. If they're already part of your vocabulary, we love that too! WHAT YOU'LL DO You own your clients' marketing leadership. That means: Creating quarterly strategies connected to their business goals. Participating in leadership meetings (L10s for our EOS clients) as the marketing expert. Coordinating with the teams executing the marketing work, whether that’s the amazing delivery team at Plum, your clients’ internal marketing employees, or the agencies they’ve hired. Owning the rocks (goals), the roadmap, the reporting, and the relationship Your clients will fall into three patterns: manufacturers with direct-to-consumer channels, B2B and niche manufacturing companies running on EOS, and second-generation owners modernizing legacy brands. ABOUT YOU You probably look something like this: Five to eight years in account management, brand leadership, agency operations, or in-house marketing director roles A histo

Trusted by global leaders like Canva, HubSpot, Tripadvisor, Bosch, and Deutsche Telekom , we’re building the retrieval infrastructure layer for modern AI. Recently raising $50M in Series B funding, we are growing rapidly and committed to transforming how AI understands and interacts with data. As a remote-first company, we believe diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences fuel innovation. Here, you’ll own meaningful work, tackle challenges, and grow alongside passionate individuals dedicated to shaping the future of AI. We are looking for a Head of Marketing who will take ownership of Qdrant's marketing function and scale it. This is a very hands-on role and you'll set strategy and run campaigns yourself while you grow the team. You report directly to the COO. You'll inherit a team of 8 and growing across growth, demand generation, PMM, and design. You'll also partner closely with our Head of DevRel and VP of Sales. Your focus will be to build programs that amplify the community and respect it, and turn organic adoption into enterprise pipeline. Not market over it. What You Will Own Build and lead the marketing org across demand generation, product marketing, field marketing, growth, and design. Own the pipeline number jointly with Sales. Stand up a demand generation engine spanning enterprise campaigns, account-based marketing (ABM), outbound coordination, competitive migrations, and field events. Drive product-led growth (PLG) acquisition and conversion. Our open-source adoption is compounding; your job is to turn it into revenue. Lead go-to-market for major product launches: positioning, packaging, pricing narrative, competitive framing, and launch execution. Build ABM programs targeting enterprise verticals (healthcare, legal, financial services, e-commerce) and competitive migration campaigns against legacy and native alternatives. Own content strategy, SEO, generative engine optimization (GEO), and technical product marketing, so Qdrant shows up where developers and enterprise buyers are researching, evaluating, and buying. Who You Are 10+ years in B2B marketing with at least 3 years leading a marketing team at a developer tools company through hyper-growth, ideally from Series B to Series C and beyond. Deep familiarity with the vector search, retrieval, or broader AI infrastructure space. You don't need to know HNSW indexing on day one, but you should be able to read our docs in a weekend and hold your own in a technical conversation with our engineering team by Monday. Proven track record building both enterprise/ABM demand gen and PLG growth motions, not just one or the other. You can review a technical blog post and make it better. Strong background in technical product marketing. You can own positioning, competitive narratives, and sales enablement for deeply technical products without leaning on engineering to translate. Hands-on operator. You'll write copy, build campaigns, brief analysts, and pull Salesforce reports yourself before you have a team to delegate to. Experience leveraging AI-driven marketing workflows and automation tools (e.g., Clay, n8n, Claud Cowork) to scale outreach, enrichment, and campaign execution, while staying close to the product and audience. Low ego, high ownership. You move fast, are comfortable in navigating ambiguity, and can operate in a remote & async organization. Why join us A remote-first, international team working on cutting-edge AI infrastructure. A competitive salary with additional perks. Flexible working hours and async-friendly culture. High ownership and real impact. Open-source, engineering-driven culture. Choose your own laptop equipment. For US-based candidates, we also offer a comprehensive benefits package including 401k match, health, dental, and vision insur

You are equally at home concepting a campaign as you are building a project plan — a rare blend of creative and operational leader who thrives on both the big idea and the details that bring it to life. Collaboration across the business: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales and People teams to ensure appropriate input and creative briefs that lend themselves to a unified brand experience across the entire customer and employee journey. Work with and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executives. Web oversight: Partner with the Digital Marketing team to deliver a high-performing, on-brand web experience, establishing and enforcing shared standards that drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and creative consistency. Mentorship & Influence: Provide creative direction and mentorship to the design team, supporting their design time while fostering a culture of innovation, excellence, experimentation and continuous learning.
